description:
You are a machine-learning engineer about to start a new experiment.  
    A raw dataset file already exists at 
 
        /home/user/source_data/iris.csv 
 
    (it is a small, comma-separated file with the first row being a header).

    Your goals:

    1. Create the project workspace directory
 
           /home/user/ml_project
 
       and inside it two sub-directories named exactly:
       • raw_data  
       • processed_data

    2. Copy the file /home/user/source_data/iris.csv into the newly
       created raw_data folder so that the destination path becomes  

           /home/user/ml_project/raw_data/iris.csv

    3. Apply the following POSIX permission bits (octal form):
       • /home/user/ml_project/raw_data                755  
       • /home/user/ml_project/raw_data/iris.csv       444  
       • /home/user/ml_project/processed_data          700

       Notes:
       • Do not change permissions on any other files or directories.  
       • You do NOT need root; standard `chmod`/`mkdir` commands are
         sufficient for this task.

    4. Produce a concise audit file that the automated grader can verify.
       Create a plain-text file named

           /home/user/ml_project/permissions_report.log

       whose content is EXACTLY three lines, one per path listed below, in
       this precise order and format:

           /home/user/ml_project/raw_data 755
           /home/user/ml_project/raw_data/iris.csv 444
           /home/user/ml_project/processed_data 700

       Requirements for permissions_report.log:
       • Use a single space between the full path and the octal number.
       • No leading or trailing spaces on any line.
       • A single trailing newline at the very end of the file (standard
         POSIX text file convention).
       • No additional lines or text.

    The entire task should be achievable with only three to four standard
    shell commands (e.g., mkdir, cp, chmod, echo or printf > file).  Feel
    free to inspect your work using commands such as `ls -l`, but be sure
    your final deliverable matches the specification above before you
    finish.
truth:
Initial state supplied by the grading environment
    -----------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/source_data already exists.
    2. File /home/user/source_data/iris.csv exists with the following
       exact contents (4 lines, Unix line endings):

           sepal_length,sepal_width,petal_length,petal_width,species
           5.1,3.5,1.4,0.2,setosa
           7.0,3.2,4.7,1.4,versicolor
           6.3,3.3,6.0,2.5,virginica

       Its initial permission bits are 644.  This file must remain
       unchanged at that location.

    Expected final state for verification
    -------------------------------------
    1. Directories and files:
       • /home/user/ml_project                     (existence required)
       • /home/user/ml_project/raw_data            (existence + 755)
       • /home/user/ml_project/raw_data/iris.csv   (existence + 444)
       • /home/user/ml_project/processed_data      (existence + 700)
       • /home/user/ml_project/permissions_report.log (see below)
    2. /home/user/ml_project/raw_data/iris.csv must be byte-for-byte
       identical to /home/user/source_data/iris.csv.
    3. /home/user/ml_project/processed_data must be empty (no files).
    4. /home/user/ml_project/permissions_report.log must contain exactly
       the three lines shown in the public task description, with a single
       trailing newline and nothing else.
    5. No other permissions or ownerships should be altered anywhere in
       /home/user or its sub-directories.

    The grader will:
       a. Verify that the directory and file structures exist.
       b. Confirm the octal permission bits with `stat -c %a`.
       c. Compare the MD5 checksums of the two iris.csv files.
       d. Validate that permissions_report.log is byte-for-byte identical
          to:

              /home/user/ml_project/raw_data 755\n
              /home/user/ml_project/raw_data/iris.csv 444\n
              /home/user/ml_project/processed_data 700\n

          (where \n represents a newline, and the file ends immediately
          after the final newline).
